contextBlurb: Arizona legislators are actively debating a broad sweep of AI and data privacy measures, with standout proposals like HB2737 targeting chatbot data practices and multiple bills (SB1815, SB1790) pushing new consumer data rights requirements. On the infrastructure side, the state is weighing how to handle data center growth through tax incentives and cost-responsibility frameworks, raising questions about who bears grid and ratepayer burdens. None of these measures have yet been enacted, leaving Arizona without firm guardrails as both industry investment and legislative pressure mount.
